evidence.query: HarmonyOS ArkTS/ArkUI platform fact check. API level: 22. Stage goal: verify exact local API usage while implementing. Focus: Whether the ArkUI page lifecycle hook onPageShow fires when returning to a page via router.back() (back-navigation), so a per-page refresh can re-snapshot shared singleton repository state. Task constraint: Cross-page match-count sync: when the user adds/removes a pantry item on the Pantry page (or clears pantry on Settings, or toggles a favorite on a recipe card) and then navigates back, the Discover/Search/Saved/RecipeDetail pages must re-snapshot the shared repository singletons and recompute match counts. Plan mandates each consuming page call viewModel.refresh() in onPageShow.. Project evidence: Pages are @Entry @Component structs navigated via router.pushUrl/router.back() (HarmonyOS router API). Each page binds an @Observed ViewModel. There is no shared reactive store; instead plan introduces singleton repositories (PantryRepository/FavoritesRepository) whose snapshot() must be re-read on page re-entry. Need to confirm onPageShow is the correct lifecycle hook for re-entry refresh on router.back(), and whether aboutToAppear also fires on back (it should not).. Platform surfaces: ArkUI page lifecycle (aboutToAppear/aboutToDisappear/onPageShow/onPageHide); router.back() navigation; @Entry page reactivation. Extra constraints: Offline only. Confirm onPageShow is invoked on router.back() re-entry to a previously-pushed page, and that aboutToAppear is NOT re-invoked on back. If onPageShow does not fire on back-nav, identify the correct alternative hook.. evidence.response:
- exact_api: `onPageShow()` 
- exact_import: `import router from '@system.router';`  or `this.getUIContext().getRouter().back()` 
- lifecycle_threading: `onPageShow` is triggered each time a router-managed page (decorated with `@Entry`) is displayed, including when returning via `router.back()`.   The `aboutToAppear` lifecycle callback is NOT triggered when returning to an existing page via `router.back()`. 
- local_validation: `onPageShow` is the correct lifecycle hook for re-entry refresh on `router.back()`.  
- context_permission: `onPageShow` is available since API version 11.  The API level 22 specified in the prompt supports this. 
- return_error: `router.back()` can optionally take `BackRouterOptions` to return to a specific page and pass parameters.  Parameters can be retrieved in `onPageShow` using `this.getUIContext().getRouter().getParams()`. 
- local_forbidden: Do not change state variables in `aboutToDisappear` as it can cause unexpected errors.  Avoid time-consuming operations in `onPageShow` to ensure smooth UI responsiveness. 
- extra:page_destruction: When `router.back()` is used to return to a specified page, all pages from the original top page (included) to the specified page (excluded) are popped out of the stack and destroyed.

evidence.response:
- exact_api: `onPageShow()` 
- exact_import: `import { router } from '@kit.ArkUI';` 
- lifecycle_threading: `onPageShow()` is triggered each time a router-managed page (decorated with `@Entry`) is displayed, including when returning via `router.back()`.  `aboutToAppear()` is not re-triggered when returning to an existing page using `router.back()`. 
- local_validation: The plan to call `viewModel.refresh()` in `onPageShow()` is valid for re-snapshotting shared singleton repository state upon page re-entry via `router.back()`.  
- planned_fallback: `slot: none`

## Notes

The ArkUI page lifecycle hook `onPageShow()` is indeed the correct place to refresh data when returning to a page via `router.back()`.   This is because `onPageShow()` is triggered every time an `@Entry` decorated page is displayed, including when navigating back to it.  Conversely, `aboutToAppear()` is not re-invoked when `router.back()` is used to return to an already existing page in the stack, as the page is not re-created. 

The `router.back()` method can also pass parameters to the target page, which can be retrieved in the `onPageShow()` lifecycle callback using `this.getUIContext().getRouter().getParams()`.  This further supports the use of `onPageShow()` for re-snapshotting shared state.

View this search on DeepWiki: https://deepwiki.com/search/harmonyos-arktsarkui-platform_9d4ac139-4dc1-4d54-865a-b0211290cbd5
